message:
fix(plan): RAII join guard and timer-driver-aware sleep in plan fixtures

SharedLabHandle::wait_for_quiescent joiner path now wraps the
TaskHandle in an RAII Guard that restores the handle to the shared slot
and reverts the join state to Empty on drop. This prevents a lost handle
if the join future is cancelled or panics between taking the handle and
storing the result, which would leave subsequent joiners permanently
blocked on a handle that no longer exists.

Both wait_for_quiescent and spawn_lab_race now query the Cx for a
timer_driver to determine the current time, falling back to wall_now()
only when no timer driver is available. This ensures that sleeps in
virtual-time lab environments use the correct clock domain rather than
unconditionally sleeping based on wall time, which could either hang
indefinitely (virtual time never advances fast enough) or fire
instantly (wall time already past the deadline).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
